title: `links edit` — edit an existing link's properties
description: Implement `links edit <label> [options]` to modify any property of an existing link in-place. Complements `links add --update` by providing a focused, flag-driven editing experience without having to re-specify unchanged fields.

features: Find the link by label (case-insensitive) via findConfig() + readConfig(). If not found, exit 1: 'No link with label "<label>" found.'
Supported options (all optional — only specified flags are changed, everything else is left untouched):
--label <new-label>: rename the link. If a link with the new label already exists, exit 1: 'A link with label "<new-label>" already exists.'
--url <url>: update the URL. Validate with validateUrl() — exit 1 if invalid.
--from <date>: set or update scheduled_from. Validate with validateDate() — exit 1 if invalid.
--until <date>: set or update scheduled_until. Validate with validateDate() — exit 1 if invalid.
If no options are provided at all, print usage hint and exit 0: 'Usage: links edit <label> [--url <url>] [--label <new>] [--icon <emoji>] [--description <text>] [--from <date>] [--until <date>]'
After applying changes, write back via writeConfig() and print: '✓ Updated: <label>' (use new label if renamed, show both if renamed: '✓ Renamed "old" → "new" and updated.')
Register the edit command in src/cli.js

notes: Commander's --no-<flag> negation syntax automatically creates a boolean false value for the flag. Use opts.icon === false to detect --no-icon (as opposed to opts.icon being undefined meaning flag not passed). Same pattern for --no-description, --no-from, --no-until. Check for this carefully — the distinction between 'not passed' and 'explicitly removed' is load-bearing.
